Title: A dual role of Arabidopsis PAL nuclear localization in fine tuning flavonoid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PAL family; aromatic amino acid ammonia-lyase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream entry step supplying flavonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedlings/root-associated tissues assayed; whole seedlings for metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant UV protection and stress adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in Arabidopsis seedlings; PAL1-GFP showed notable nuclear accumulation in root meristem and elongation zone after kfb4m stabilization and after UV-B, sucrose, or naringenin treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV-B; high sucrose; exogenous naringenin (NAR); cycloheximide recovery assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression; phospho-mutant analysis; confocal localization; nuclear-cytoplasmic fractionation; Phos-tag phosphorylation assay; PAL activity assay; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; metabolite profiling; Co-IP; BiFC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed step in phenylpropanoid pathway; key control point supported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Nuclear localization of PAL1 suppresses flavonoid biosynthetic gene expression, reduces anthocyanin and flavonol accumulation, and lowers PAL enzymatic activity by nuclear sequestration.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Phosphoproteomics/mass spectrometry for phosphorylation site identification; targeted LC-MS/UPLC-QQQ-MS metabolite profiling; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering PAL localization or phosphorylation state could fine-tune flavonoid output and avoid overaccumulation; phosphosite editing or localization control may be useful but may also affect broader phenylpropanoid flux.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible reduction of overall phenylpropanoid flux and associated metabolites such as lignin or salicylic acid if PAL activity is excessively suppressed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAL1 acts not only as enzyme but also as feedback regulator after phosphorylation-dependent nuclear import; NAR used as representative metabolite cue rather than proven direct sensor ligand.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL1 is a nucleus-capable phenylpropanoid entry enzyme in Arabidopsis. 2. Elevated flavonoid status induced by UV-B, sucrose, or naringenin promotes PAL1 phosphorylation and nuclear import. 3. Nuclear PAL1 lowers catalytic activity and represses flavonoid biosynthetic gene expression, reducing anthocyanin and flavonol accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PAL family; aromatic amino acid ammonia-lyase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream entry step supplying flavonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant UV protection and stress adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NAR and UV-B promote nuclear accumulation of PAL2; present in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Confocal localization; nuclear-cytoplasmic fractionation; Co-IP interaction context with TT8
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed step in phenylpropanoid pathway; key control point supported at family level
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PAL2 also undergoes flavonoid-induced nuclear accumulation, supporting reduced cytoplasmic PAL pool and feedback suppression of flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PAL2 may be co-targeted with PAL1 to modulate phenylpropanoid entry flux via localization control.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression may broadly affect phenylpropanoid-derived traits.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAL2 is one of the major PAL enzymes in Arabidopsis; family-level activity strongly reduced in pal1/2 mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL2 behaves similarly to PAL1 in flavonoid-induced nuclear relocalization. 2. PAL2 interacts with TT8 in vivo. 3. PAL2 contributes to the feedback-controlled PAL pool influencing flavonoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PAL family; aromatic amino acid ammonia-lyase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream entry step supplying flavonoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant UV protection and stress adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NAR and UV-B promote nuclear accumulation of PAL4
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Confocal localization; nuclear-cytoplasmic fractionation; Co-IP interaction context with TT8
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed step in phenylpropanoid pathway; key control point supported at family level
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PAL4 shows inducible nuclear accumulation consistent with PAL-mediated feedback repression of flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for modulating phenylpropanoid entry flux alongside PAL1/PAL2.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAL4, unlike PAL3, responds strongly to NAR-induced nuclear import.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL4 undergoes flavonoid-induced nuclear translocation. 2. PAL4 can associate with TT8 in vivo. 3. PAL4 supports the broader PAL-family feedback mechanism controlling flavonoid production.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: bHLH transcription factor; MBW complex component
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant UV protection and stress adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as MBW complex component; activity inhibited by PAL binding that disrupts complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates flavonoid biosynthetic genes as part of MBW complex; PAL binding suppresses TT8-dependent activation of CHI and FLS and reduces MBW formation with MYB75
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with MYB75 and TTG1; interaction with nuclear PAL disrupts MBW assembly
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core MBW component required for high flavonoid gene expression and directly targeted by nuclear PAL feedback
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC; Co-IP in Nicotiana benthamiana and Arabidopsis; dual-luciferase assay; interaction interference assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nuclear PAL accumulation under UV-B, sucrose, or NAR indirectly affects TT8 activity
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Flavonoid accumulation status via PAL nuclear translocation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in metabolite-triggered negative feedback through PAL-TT8 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TT8-driven activation of flavonoid biosynthesis is attenuated when nuclear PAL binds TT8 and disrupts MBW complex formation, lowering flavonoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for TT8-GFP/TT8-HA constructs in assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating TT8 interaction interfaces or PAL-insensitive TT8 variants may sustain flavonoid production under feedback conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Hyperactivation may cause excessive flavonoid accumulation and broader transcriptional changes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TT8 was the only tested flavonoid MBW factor showing robust interaction with PAL; TTG1 and MYB75 did not directly interact with PAL in assays.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT8 physically interacts with PAL in planta. 2. Nuclear PAL disrupts TT8-MYB75 association and diminishes TT8-mediated promoter activation. 3. TT8 is the regulatory node through which PAL exerts transcriptional feedback on flavonoid biosynthesis.
